Heavy tab-switch loop across three representative scenes to catch drift in repeated scene swaps.
Long-running animation tick loop for drift detection across a 600-frame idle window.
Pathological retained-tree mount of ten thousand flat rectangles.
Three hundred simultaneous animated bars sharing one encode pass to trap bulk animation regressions.
Hundred-hertz ticker update loop on the stress scene to trap high-frequency mutation regressions.

Location bridge overhead: permission-gated sensor ingest plus snapshot materialization.
Bluetooth bridge overhead: discovery cache update and snapshot emission without renderer work.

App-owned share bridge path from selected records to a prepared share payload summary, excluding the system share sheet presentation.
App-owned localhost JSON transport path from bytes-ready through decode into the first rendered feed skeleton, excluding network stack and system UI.
App-owned localhost image transport path from bytes-ready through decode into the first rendered hero image, excluding network stack and system UI.

layers.label: Engine Microbenchmarks
notes: Engine coverage currently spans system hot paths, primitive views, animations, primitive lifecycle slices, and author-facing APIs.

layers.label: Representative Screen Flows
notes: Flow coverage now spans offscreen launch/lifecycle, router scenes, and explicit user journeys, but hitch and device refresh-mode batteries are still incomplete.

layers.label: OS-Bridge Benchmarks
notes: Bridge coverage currently measures only app-owned wrapper overhead, not system-owned surface cost as a renderer win.

battery.label: Launch & Lifecycle
notes: Offscreen bootstrap now includes simple-home and heavy-home cold launch, route-driven detail launch, warm resume, and foreground-after-background lifecycle workloads.

battery.label: Primitive Mount / Update / Destroy
notes: Flat rects, labels, cards, images, an empty-root slice, a shared control-set slice, and retained-tree remove-all/remount slices are all covered.

battery.label: Layout & Invalidation
notes: Flat-grid rotation, deep-stack theme swap, and safe-area inset relayout batteries are all implemented.

battery.label: Text & Text Input
notes: Large-editor keystroke, paste, and selection-replace workloads now complement the existing text-field and input-form coverage.

battery.label: Image Pipeline
notes: The committed image battery now splits PNG decode, Metal texture upload, and first-visible presentation into separate persisted workloads.

battery.label: Lists, Grids, & Chat
notes: Feed, thumbnail-grid, and chat-thread scroll matrices now exist alongside the collection encode and navigation slices.

battery.label: Navigation & Input Latency
notes: Direct button-press, slider-scrub, and text-focus response batteries now complement the higher-level journey cases.

battery.label: Animation & Visual Effects
notes: Representative animations exist, but there is no dedicated hitch-ratio or refresh-mode matrix yet for 60 Hz versus native refresh.

battery.label: State Mutation & Reconciliation
notes: Single-node, 1 percent, 10 percent, and full-theme tree mutation batteries now expose diff/apply cost directly.

battery.label: OS Bridge Overhead
notes: Permission, sensor, photo import, file import, share payload, and localhost transport/render bridge workloads are all covered without claiming system-owned UI as a renderer win.

battery.label: Endurance, Memory, & Thermal Drift
notes: Open/close, tab-switch, and idle-animation endurance loops are now part of the committed Oxide battery.

battery.label: Stress & Pathological Regressions
notes: Dedicated 10k-node, 300-animation, and 100 Hz ticker traps now complement the router stress scene.
The Oxide report is intentionally explicit about missing contract families so the battery does not over-claim comprehensiveness.

Current Oxide coverage now spans engine hot paths, launch/lifecycle, representative scenes, and bridge slices; the biggest remaining gaps are hitch-oriented flow metrics and real-device refresh-mode coverage.

findings.summary: DrawListBuilder::clear now clears retained vertex and index storage, eliminating stale geometry accumulation when builders are reused across frames.
ui-core::prepare_draws now keeps cumulative clip intersections on the stack instead of rebuilding the full stack on every ClipPop.
ui-core::coalesce_adjacent_draws now uses a single linear compaction pass instead of Vec::remove-based quadratic merging.
renderer-metal still encodes rounded rectangles one draw at a time with per-draw parameter binding; that remains the clearest GPU-side batching opportunity on real Metal targets.

The macOS glyph indirect-command-buffer path is now default-disabled because Metal validation exposed CPU access to private ICB storage and an invalid ICB pipeline configuration; restoring it with a truly valid text ICB path remains a high-value GPU follow-up.
Label wrapping still re-shapes tentative strings per word and clones intermediate Strings, which is likely the next CPU hotspot for text-heavy wrapped layouts.
